questions.question: Why does a template ref return null when used with v-if?
options: v-if and ref are incompatible directives in Vue 3
The element is removed from the DOM when v-if is false, and Vue sets the ref to null
Template refs only work with v-show, not v-if
The ref is not initialized until the user interacts with the element

questions.question: What is the advantage of using v-show over v-if when you need persistent template ref access?
options: v-show creates a separate reactive context for the element
v-show keeps the element in the DOM (hidden via CSS), so the ref is never null
v-show is processed before v-if, ensuring the ref is set first
v-show automatically initializes the ref to an empty HTMLElement

questions.question: In Vue 3.5+, what API provides cleaner template ref handling with better TypeScript inference?
